target.name: Harbinger
target.founder: John Harris, Phillip Weicker, Will Eberts

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Harbinger is a Series C transportation company that has raised $358M to date. The comp universe for frontier-transport ventures includes 7 private peers (Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Slate Auto, ZeroAvia) with no public comparables, reflecting the nascent stage of the sector.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($358M) as a proxy base and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C advancement yields an implied valuation of $170M. This method was selected because peer-median analysis was not viable: while 3 same-sector transportation peers exist, none satisfied the filters for stage-adjacency or valuation-tier comparability needed to construct a sufficiently robust peer set (minimum 3 required).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $144M to $195M, with a median point of $170M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological anchor; key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.7x multiplier for Harbinger's specific risk profile relative to broader Series C stage norms, and the absence of transaction or revenue data to calibrate alternative approaches. Given frontier-tech sector volatility and the sparse comparable landscape, valuation confidence should be weighted accordingly.
